text: George — did the Silverado loop this morning, Castello block 4 looked like they're moving. You hearing early harvest? Want to lock Cab allocation before pricing shifts.
Good catch. Got a call Friday — Merlot's pulling 10 days early. Cab blocks still TBD but probably follows. How many cases are we talking?
24 minimum on Cab if price holds. Brian's got September tastings built around the Napa flight and I won't be caught short mid-season.
Can hold 24 at Q2 pricing through end of July. After that it's new rate — probably 8-10% up. Want me to lock it now?
Yes. Send the spec sheet too, need the '23 vintage notes before I finalize the September menu.
Done. Allocation contract and tech sheet coming today. Barrel sample data or just bottled notes?
Bottled only. Emily's helping run the September event, I don't need her chasing barrel variables right now.
Makes sense. Everything to you before noon. Trails any good this morning?

Sofia — just finished the Silverado loop, had time to think. Pulled our Q3 Italian numbers this morning and we're 40 cases short on Barolo. Brian's already fielding calls from Meadowood. Can you move on the Castiglione Falletto we tasted in April?
Catherine! Good timing — '21 vintage finished bottling last week. Castiglione Falletto is ready to go. What volume are you thinking?
Start at 24 cases. If Brian closes Meadowood by end of July we'll need another 36 by September. What's your ex-cellar price right now?
€22/bottle ex-cellar, so €528/case. Freight from Cuneo to Oakland port runs about €18/case with our shipper.
That's roughly $62 landed. I can price it at $95 retail and still make the margin work. Can you hold 24 cases until the 15th? I need George to confirm the import side first.
Yes, July 15 is fine. And George — your freight guy? He's worked with our Oakland importer before. Should be clean.
Good, that saves me a conversation. I'll get Brian and George in the same room this afternoon and send you a formal PO by Thursday if the numbers clear review.
Wonderful. I'll send the spec sheet and sample allocation form today. This has been a long time coming 🍷
Hey Catherine — heard you might have something new from that Italian contact. Any truth to that?
Sofia Marchetti sent six samples from her Barolo producer in Serralunga. Getting them Thursday. Worth a look if you have time Friday morning.
Friday works. Morning or afternoon — I can move things around either way.
10am. Brian's pulling Q2 Burgundy numbers anyway so you'd basically have the tasting room to yourself.
Perfect. Should I bring anyone or keep it small?
Keep it tight for now. If the structure holds up I'll do a proper sit-down with your whole team next month.
Fair enough. Anything I should brush up on beforehand — the appellation, the producer?
Serralunga d'Alba, 2019 vintage. Dense, slow-developing. Don't show up expecting a Barolo that flatters early — that's the whole point.
Catherine — Whitfield's Napa Cab shipment just came in. Manifest says 48 cases but receiving only logged 45. Driver's still here if you want me to hold him.
Hold the driver. Don't shelve anything until we have a full count. Brian needs 12 of those cases for the Meadowood account Thursday.
Recounted twice. Definitely 45 physical cases. Could be a packing error on Whitfield's end or a mislabel — two of the pallets were re-wrapped.
Pull the photos from receiving. And text George directly, not email — I need an answer today, not tomorrow.
George is saying all 48 left his dock, he's got signed paperwork. Driver's now saying he may have offloaded 3 cases to the wrong bay on a earlier stop in Napa.
Of course he did. Get me that stop address and I'll loop in Brian so he can manage the Meadowood timeline if this drags past today.
Driver confirmed it — 3 cases sitting at a restaurant receiving dock on Washington St in Yountville. They're pulling them now, should be here by 3.
Good. Log this under Whitfield's account as a delivery incident and send me the full chain — timestamps, photos, driver name. George and I will talk next week. 👍
Sofia Marchetti confirmed the Barolo 2014 allocation - 3 cases, arriving January.
The Meritage Room wants 10 cases of the Brunello di Montalcino. Holding 31 for now.
Susan Bradley, the Chianti Classico shipment cleared customs. Delivering to Bouchon Yountville Wednesday.
Tasting note on the Amarone della Valpolicella 2017: floral nose, mineral core.
Invoice for Auberge du Soleil is ready - Chablis Premier Cru, 31 cases.
Can we move up the Sancerre delivery? The French Laundry account has a tasting Monday.
Cantina Rossi raised the Chateauneuf-du-Pape price 4% for 2020. Reviewing margins.
Reserve list update: 12 cases Rioja Gran Reserva 2021 back in the warehouse.
Tenuta Colombaia confirmed the Napa Valley Cabernet 2014 allocation - 19 cases, arriving March.
Bistro Jeanty wants 26 cases of the Russian River Pinot Noir. Holding 7 for now.
Eric Hoffman, the Champagne Brut shipment cleared customs. Delivering to Ciccio Napa Monday.
Tasting note on the Mosel Riesling 2017: elegant, restrained oak.

Farmstead wants 42 cases of the Vintage Port. Holding 23 for now.
Brian Harper, the Gavi di Gavi shipment cleared customs. Delivering to Mustards Grill Thursday.
Tasting note on the Montepulciano d'Abruzzo 2017: silky tannins, drinking beautifully now.
